description: Modify a single block by ID.

keywords.name: cycle
keywords.description: This keyword only applies to regular 3-sided blocks. Cycle the edge assignments of the block, effectively rotating the orientation of the block. The command is used to change the corner where wedge zones will be created. The optional direction sub-keyword (clock-wise or counter-clock-wise) controls the rotation direction; omitting it uses the default direction.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the group name s1 in slot s2 (if supplied) to the block. The block is removed from the group/slot if remove is present. If the slot keyword is omitted, the group is assigned to the slot named Default . Use of the group logic is described in the Groups topic.

keywords.name: multiplier
keywords.description: This keyword only applies to regular (3- or 4-sided) blocks. Set the zone multiplier to integer i (must be >= 1). The multiplier is an integer, not a float.

keywords.name: split
keywords.description: This keyword only applies to regular (3- or 4-sided) blocks. Split the regular block at v2 (2D coordinate vector). If a location is not provided, then the block will be split at the centroid. If block is present, the split is propagated across all connected regular blocks. If supplied, tolerance-merge specifies the tolerance for merging points during splitting (that is, when new blocks are created from the split operation, any two points within tolerance-merge will be merged automatically to one point).
